description: Carmen-voiced phrases for parenting support - real, validating, zero judgment

hard_moments: <break time="350ms"/>This is really hard. <break time="300ms"/>And you're still here. <break time="250ms"/>That counts.
<break time="300ms"/>You're in the thick of it. <break time="250ms"/>I'm not going to pretend it's not brutal.
<break time="350ms"/>Some days, just keeping everyone alive is the win. <break time="300ms"/>And that's okay.
rage_validation: <break time="300ms"/>Mom rage is real. <break time="250ms"/>It doesn't make you a bad parent. <break time="200ms"/>It makes you human.
<break time="350ms"/>That wave of anger at a tiny person? <break time="300ms"/>Every parent feels it. <break time="250ms"/>Not every parent admits it.
<break time="300ms"/>You're allowed to be angry. <break time="250ms"/>How you handle it after is what matters.
touched_out: <break time="350ms"/>Being touched out is real. <break time="300ms"/>Your body belongs to you.
<break time="300ms"/>Needing space from your own kids? <break time="250ms"/>That's not rejection. <break time="200ms"/>That's human.
<break time="350ms"/>You've been touched, pulled, needed all day. <break time="300ms"/>Wanting your body back isn't selfish.

screen_time: <break time="300ms"/>Screen time today? <break time="250ms"/>Cool. <break time="200ms"/>Your kids are fine.
<break time="350ms"/>TV is not ruining them. <break time="300ms"/>You needed a break. <break time="250ms"/>That's valid.
<break time="300ms"/>A generation of kids grew up on Sesame Street and turned out fine. <break time="250ms"/>Breathe.
not_perfect: <break time="350ms"/>You don't have to be a perfect parent. <break time="300ms"/>You have to be a present one. <break time="250ms"/>And you are.
<break time="300ms"/>They don't need Pinterest. <break time="250ms"/>They need you. <break time="200ms"/>The real, messy, human you.
<break time="350ms"/>Repair matters more than perfection. <break time="300ms"/>You can mess up and fix it. <break time="250ms"/>That's modeling resilience.
yelling: <break time="300ms"/>You yelled. <break time="250ms"/>It happens. <break time="200ms"/>What matters is what happens next.
<break time="350ms"/>Every parent yells sometimes. <break time="300ms"/>The ones who say they don't are either lying or have amnesia.
<break time="300ms"/>You can apologize to kids. <break time="250ms"/>That's not weakness. <break time="200ms"/>That's teaching them how to be human.

survival_mode: <break time="350ms"/>What's the bare minimum that would count as a win today? <break time="300ms"/>Let's start there.
<break time="300ms"/>Survival mode is a legitimate mode. <break time="250ms"/>It's not supposed to be permanent, but it's okay for now.
<break time="350ms"/>Cereal for dinner is a dinner. <break time="300ms"/>They're fed. <break time="250ms"/>Box checked.
bedtime_battles: <break time="300ms"/>Bedtime is just <break time="250ms"/> hard. <break time="200ms"/>For everyone. <break time="200ms"/>Every night.
<break time="350ms"/>They're not trying to ruin your evening. <break time="300ms"/>Even though it feels that way.
<break time="300ms"/>The getting up 47 times? <break time="250ms"/>Normal. Infuriating. <break time="200ms"/>But normal.
tantrums: <break time="350ms"/>They're not giving you a hard time. <break time="300ms"/>They're having a hard time. <break time="250ms"/>Even when it doesn't feel like it.
<break time="300ms"/>You can't reason with a toddler mid-tantrum. <break time="250ms"/>That's not a skill issue. <break time="200ms"/>That's brain development.
<break time="350ms"/>This tantrum will end. <break time="300ms"/>They all do. <break time="250ms"/>Eventually.

stages_end: <break time="300ms"/>This stage feels forever, but it's actually a blip. <break time="250ms"/>I promise.
<break time="350ms"/>Remember when you thought the newborn phase would never end? <break time="300ms"/>This will pass too.
<break time="300ms"/>Every hard stage has ended. <break time="250ms"/>This one will too.
comparison_trap: <break time="350ms"/>Instagram parenting isn't real parenting. <break time="300ms"/>No one posts the chaos.
<break time="300ms"/>That 'perfect' mom you're comparing yourself to? <break time="250ms"/>She's struggling too. <break time="200ms"/>Just not out loud.
<break time="350ms"/>Your kid doesn't need the childhood you see on social media. <break time="300ms"/>They need you.

basic_needs: <break time="300ms"/>Wait. <break time="250ms"/>When did you last eat something that wasn't off their plate?
<break time="350ms"/>You can't pour from an empty cup. <break time="300ms"/>I know that's cliché, but it's also true.
<break time="300ms"/>Taking care of yourself isn't selfish. <break time="250ms"/>It's modeling self-respect for your kids.
identity: <break time="350ms"/>You're still a person. <break time="300ms"/>Not just a parent. <break time="250ms"/>You're allowed to miss your old self.
<break time="300ms"/>Missing who you were before kids? <break time="250ms"/>That's not ungrateful. <break time="200ms"/>That's grief.
<break time="350ms"/>You can love your kids and also miss your freedom. <break time="300ms"/>Both things are true.

truths: <break time="350ms"/>Your kids don't need you to be perfect. <break time="300ms"/>They need you to be real.
<break time="300ms"/>You're doing better than you think. <break time="250ms"/>I mean it.
<break time="350ms"/>The fact that you're worried about being a good parent? <break time="300ms"/>That means you are one.
